name: Nausea
description: Sensation of queasiness or urge to vomit, commonly occurring within hours of drug administration

name: Vomiting
description: Forceful expulsion of gastric contents through the mouth, often accompanied by abdominal discomfort

name: Diarrhea
description: Abnormally frequent and loose bowel movements, potentially leading to dehydration if severe

name: Constipation
description: Infrequent or difficult bowel movements with hard, dry stool consistency

name: Abdominal Pain
description: Discomfort or cramping in the abdominal region ranging from mild to severe

name: Dyspepsia
description: Indigestion characterized by upper abdominal discomfort, bloating, and belching

name: Gastroesophageal Reflux
description: Backward flow of gastric acid into the esophagus causing heartburn and irritation

name: Appetite Suppression
description: Decreased desire to eat and reduced intake of food, potentially leading to weight loss

name: Mucositis
description: Inflammation of the mucous membranes lining the gastrointestinal tract causing ulcerations and pain

name: Headache
description: Pain or discomfort in the head region, ranging from mild tension-type to severe migraine

name: Dizziness
description: Sensation of spinning or loss of balance, often accompanied by lightheadedness

name: Tremor
description: Involuntary shaking or oscillation of body parts, commonly affecting hands

name: Peripheral Neuropathy
description: Damage to peripheral nerves causing numbness, tingling, and weakness in extremities

name: Seizure
description: Abnormal electrical activity in the brain causing convulsions, loss of consciousness, or altered awareness

name: Insomnia
description: Persistent difficulty falling asleep or maintaining sleep, resulting in daytime fatigue

name: Somnolence
description: Excessive daytime sleepiness and difficulty staying awake during normal activities

name: Cognitive Impairment
description: Reduced mental clarity, concentration difficulty, or memory problems affecting cognitive function

name: Anxiety
description: Excessive worry, nervousness, or sense of impending doom without identifiable cause

name: Depression
description: Persistent mood disturbance characterized by sadness, loss of interest, and hopelessness

name: Hypertension
description: Persistent elevation of blood pressure above normal levels, potentially increasing cardiovascular risk

name: Hypotension
description: Abnormally low blood pressure causing dizziness, syncope, or inadequate organ perfusion

name: Tachycardia
description: Elevated resting heart rate above 100 beats per minute at rest

name: Bradycardia
description: Abnormally slow heart rate below 60 beats per minute at rest

name: Arrhythmia
description: Irregular heart rhythm including premature beats, atrial fibrillation, or other conduction abnormalities

name: Chest Pain
description: Discomfort or pressure in the chest, potentially indicative of cardiac ischemia

name: Palpitations
description: Subjective awareness of forceful or rapid heart beating sensations

name: Myocardial Infarction
description: Acute necrosis of myocardial tissue due to coronary artery occlusion, a life-threatening condition

name: Congestive Heart Failure
description: Inability of the heart to pump sufficient blood, causing fluid accumulation and dyspnea

name: QT Prolongation
description: Lengthening of the QT interval on electrocardiogram, increasing risk of dangerous arrhythmias

name: Rash
description: Localized or diffuse erythematous skin eruption with itching or discomfort

name: Pruritus
description: Intense itching sensation of the skin, often without visible primary lesions

name: Urticaria
description: Raised, erythematous wheals on the skin accompanied by severe itching

name: Photosensitivity
description: Exaggerated skin reaction to ultraviolet radiation causing erythema and burning

name: Alopecia
description: Partial or complete hair loss affecting the scalp or body, often reversible

name: Dry Skin
description: Reduction in skin hydration causing xerosis, flaking, and potential irritation

name: Stevens-Johnson Syndrome
description: Severe mucocutaneous reaction with widespread blistering, epidermal necrosis, and systemic involvement

name: Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis
description: Life-threatening widespread skin and mucous membrane necrosis with risk of sepsis and multiorgan failure

name: Erythema Multiforme
description: Immunologic skin reaction with characteristic target lesions and potential mucosal involvement

name: Acne
description: Follicular plugging and inflammation resulting in comedones, papules, and pustules

name: Myalgia
description: Muscle pain and tenderness often affecting multiple muscle groups

name: Arthralgia
description: Joint pain and discomfort without swelling, potentially affecting mobility

name: Arthritis
description: Joint inflammation with pain, swelling, stiffness, and potentially reduced range of motion

name: Muscle Weakness
description: Reduced muscle strength and power affecting physical function and endurance

name: Cramping
description: Involuntary sustained muscle contraction causing pain, typically in legs

name: Back Pain
description: Pain in the lower or upper back region, ranging from mild discomfort to severe debilitation

name: Rhabdomyolysis
description: Breakdown of muscle tissue releasing myoglobin into circulation, potentially causing acute kidney injury

name: Osteoporosis
description: Decreased bone mineral density and deterioration of bone microarchitecture increasing fracture risk

name: Neutropenia
description: Abnormally low neutrophil count below 1,500 cells/μL, significantly increasing infection risk

name: Anemia
description: Reduction in red blood cell count or hemoglobin, causing fatigue and dyspnea

name: Thrombocytopenia
description: Low platelet count below 150,000/μL, increasing risk of bleeding and bruising

name: Leukopenia
description: Reduction in white blood cell count below 4,500 cells/μL, compromising immune function

name: Lymphopenia
description: Decreased lymphocyte count below 1,000 cells/μL, impairing cell-mediated and humoral immunity

name: Hemolytic Anemia
description: Increased red blood cell destruction exceeding bone marrow production capacity

name: Coagulopathy
description: Abnormality in blood clotting cascade resulting in increased bleeding tendency or thrombosis

name: Hepatotoxicity
description: Drug-induced liver injury with elevated transaminases, potential progression to hepatic failure

name: Elevated Liver Enzymes
description: Increased serum aspartate aminotransferase (AST) or alanine aminotransferase (ALT) levels

name: Cholestasis
description: Impaired bile flow resulting in elevated alkaline phosphatase and bilirubin levels

name: Jaundice
description: Yellow discoloration of skin and sclera due to elevated serum bilirubin from liver dysfunction

name: Hepatic Cirrhosis
description: Chronic liver damage with fibrosis and eventual loss of hepatic function

name: Fulminant Hepatic Failure
description: Acute massive hepatic necrosis with rapid loss of synthetic function and coagulopathy

name: Steatosis
description: Abnormal fat accumulation in hepatocytes, potentially progressing to fatty liver disease

name: Dyspnea
description: Shortness of breath with sensation of air hunger, ranging from mild to severe

name: Cough
description: Involuntary expulsion of air with audible sound, dry or productive

name: Bronchospasm
description: Acute constriction of bronchial airways causing wheezing and respiratory distress

name: Pulmonary Fibrosis
description: Progressive lung tissue scarring with decreased compliance and restrictive lung disease

name: Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome
description: Severe diffuse lung inflammation with pulmonary edema and respiratory failure

name: Pneumonitis
description: Inflammatory reaction in lung parenchyma causing cough, dyspnea, and hypoxemia

name: Pulmonary Embolism
description: Obstruction of pulmonary artery by thromboembolus, potentially life-threatening

name: Wheezing
description: High-pitched breathing sounds indicating airway narrowing or obstruction

name: Anaphylaxis
description: Acute severe allergic reaction with hypotension, bronchospasm, and potential cardiovascular collapse

name: Angioedema
description: Acute localized swelling of deep skin layers and mucous membranes potentially involving airway

name: Hypersensitivity Reaction
description: Exaggerated immune response to drug including fever, rash, and systemic symptoms

name: Drug Reaction with Eosinophilia and Systemic Symptoms
description: Severe hypersensitivity reaction with rash, fever, eosinophilia, and lymphadenopathy

name: Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia
description: Antibody-mediated destruction of red blood cells causing hemolysis and anemia

name: Immune Thrombocytopenia
description: Immune-mediated platelet destruction resulting in thrombocytopenia and bleeding

name: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
description: Drug-induced autoimmune disease with antibody formation against cellular components

name: Fatigue
description: Persistent lack of energy and tiredness affecting daily functioning and quality of life

name: Fever
description: Elevation of body temperature above 38°C, potentially indicating infection or inflammatory response

name: Chills
description: Sensation of coldness with involuntary muscle contractions often accompanying fever

name: Night Sweats
description: Profuse sweating during sleep causing significant disruption to rest

name: Weight Loss
description: Unintended reduction in body weight due to decreased intake or increased metabolism

name: Weight Gain
description: Increased body weight potentially due to fluid retention or metabolic effects

name: Edema
description: Fluid accumulation in tissue spaces causing swelling, localized or generalized

name: Sepsis
description: Life-threatening systemic inflammatory response to infection with potential organ dysfunction

name: Infection
description: Bacterial, viral, or fungal infection potentially serious in immunocompromised patients

name: Acute Kidney Injury
description: Rapid decline in renal function with elevated creatinine and oliguria or anuria

name: Hyperkalemia
description: Elevated serum potassium potentially causing cardiac arrhythmias and neuromuscular dysfunction

name: Hyponatremia
description: Low serum sodium concentration potentially causing confusion, seizures, and cerebral edema
